title: The Most Frequently Ordered Products for Each Customer
description: Find the most frequently ordered product(s) for each customer.
constraints: (order_id, customer_id, product_id) is the primary key of Orders
product_id is the primary key of Products
Ties on order frequency are included (all products with max count per customer)
Each customer has at least one order
key_concept: RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY customer ORDER BY COUNT DESC) = 1. Handles ties.

hints.L1: Count orders per (customer, product). Rank within customer by count descending. Keep rank = 1.
hints.L2: WITH cte AS (SELECT customer_id, product_id, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rk FROM Orders GROUP BY customer_id, product_id)
hints.L3: WITH cte AS (SELECT customer_id, product_id, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rk FROM Orders GROUP BY customer_id, product_id) SELECT c.customer_id, c.product_id, p.product_name FROM cte c JOIN Products p ON c.product_id=p.product_id WHERE c.rk=1
